text.title: visibility-of-system-status Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for visibility-of-system-status as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No loading state: user clicks button, nothing visible happens, user clicks again
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without loading state, users cannot tell if their click registered. Repeated clicks cause duplicate form submissions, duplicate API calls, or duplicate payments. The loading state is the signal that the click was received.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duration-free, content-free status messages give users no information to judge whether the wait is acceptable. "Uploading 3 of 5 files" is infinitely more useful than "Processing..."

failure-mode.description: Progress bar showing fake/frozen progress (0% to 100% in one jump, or stuck at 99%)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Progress bars showing inaccurate progress erode trust more than no progress bar at all. Only use determinate progress bars when the backend provides real progress events. Otherwise use an indeterminate spinner.

failure-mode.description: Success toast auto-dismissing before user can read it; error toast also auto-dismissing
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-dismiss on success is acceptable (3-5s is enough). Auto-dismiss on error is never acceptable — users must be able to read the error and act on it. Errors require manual dismissal.

failure-mode.description: Using aria-live="assertive" for routine status updates (e.g., "Changes saved")
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Assertive live regions interrupt the screen reader immediately, regardless of what the user is doing. Using assertive for non-critical status announcements disrupts users unnecessarily. Reserve assertive for actual errors requiring immediate response.
